name: random1st-plugins
description: Personal Claude Code plugin marketplace — reasoning, audit, and meta-workflow skills extracted from a working dev/AI stack.

plugins.name: tribunal
plugins.description: Provider-relative independent code audit. Current agent arbitrates; the two other CLIs (Claude, Codex, Gemini) review in parallel. For security-sensitive code, pre-merge gates, critical bug fixes.

plugins.name: delegate
plugins.description: Unified external AI delegation — route tasks to Codex or Gemini based on cost/quality tradeoffs. Use for second opinions, deep reviews, large-context analysis.

plugins.name: fpf
plugins.description: FPF reasoning baseline — ADI cycle (Abduction → Deduction → Induction), lifecycle stages (Explore → Shape → Evidence → Operate), I/D/S discipline, calibration tags. Applies on every non-trivial problem.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: Force verification that work is actually complete and production-ready. Triggers: 'double check', 'is it really done'. Not for trivial confirmations.

plugins.name: systematic-debugging
plugins.description: Root-cause-first debugging methodology. Reproduce, isolate, hypothesize, fix at source. Use on any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior.

plugins.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
plugins.description: Pattern for splitting independent investigation domains across parallel agents. Use when 2+ unrelated failures/tasks have no shared state.

plugins.name: scratchpad
plugins.description: Persistent working memory for multi-iteration tasks — TDD cycles, long refactors, iterate-style loops. Project-level state at .claude/scratchpad.md.

plugins.name: skill-creator
plugins.description: Guide and scripts for creating effective Claude Code skills. Includes init_skill.py, package_skill.py, and design-pattern references.

plugins.name: create-agents
plugins.description: Create Claude Code agents — autonomous workers with isolated context and restricted tools. Distinguishes agents from skills, includes templates.
